analysis.consistency: High. The data structure is consistent with typical spell definitions.
analysis.quality: High. The data is complete, containing all necessary mechanics (level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, classes).

mechanics_review.level: 1st level divination spell, appropriate for the listed classes.
mechanics_review.range: Self, appropriate for a utility/information-gathering spell.
mechanics_review.components: V, S, M (a pinch of soot and salt). Material component is simple and inexpensive, fitting for a 1st-level spell.
mechanics_review.duration: 1 hour, reasonable for allowing time to read or listen across various situations.
mechanics_review.effects: Understands spoken language heard and written language touched. The caveat about not decoding secret messages/arcane glyphs is a crucial mechanical limiter and is clearly stated.
mechanics_review.page_reading_time: 1 minute per page is a good standard for the written language effect.
mechanics_review.classes: Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard. This selection is logical for an intelligence/utility-focused divination spell.

analysis.suggested_action: Archive as is. No corrections necessary.
